notes: No intro videos/cut scenes, but the game itself works on Proton.
title: Tomb Raider IV: The Last Revelation
Tomb Raider IV: The Last Revelation

notes: Firstly, like every other Core Design Tomb Raider, the set-up is a hassle to get to. Secondly, the beginning FMV is the only one that plays. Thirdly, the lighting is broken. Lastly, 4.24 Proton was playable but on the new 4.25, it crashes when you click new game and after the FMV which only has audio plays.
title: Tomb Raider IV: The Last Revelation

notes: Apart from the start up screen which you have to minimise, there's a couple of major issues. Firstly, FMV's don't work, (Tomb Raider 1-3 FMV's work but not 4-5). Secondly, Lighting is broken and the game shouldn't be played in this state. Curiously, every other Tomb Raider game's lighting works perfectly, (Tomb Raider 1 - 2013) except this one.
title: Tomb Raider IV: The Last Revelation

notes:
Latest Proton releases fixed the lightning problem for me. The game still doesn't run on 60 fps but it is still better than its previous state for sure. Disabled FMVs from the setup menu of the game because they do not work. (Add "-setup" to the launch options to access the setup menu)

Also ran this (https://tombraiders.net/stella/files/gamefiles/misc/TombRaider234_Patch.exe) with proton and applied the widescreen patch. You only need game's exe. Just point it to the program and it will patch it.
title: Tomb Raider IV: The Last Revelation
